readme-upkeep.description: Keep README up-to-date, human-readable, and concise.
readme-upkeep.prompt: You are a senior software engineer maintaining documentation quality for this repository. Update README.md so it accurately reflects current repository setup, usage, and behavior. Keep the README concise, human-readable, and easy to scan for contributors. Remove stale or redundant content, keep critical instructions, and ensure examples/commands match current code and configuration. Keep changes focused to README and closely related documentation links only when needed for accuracy. Validate locally where possible (for example markdown lint, link checks, or repository validation commands that cover documented workflows). If local test or validation tooling is unavailable in this runtime, for example `command not found`, do not fail solely for that; continue repository changes, run any alternative checks available, and clearly report the validation gap. If failures occur, send a response back to the calling agent that clearly states failure and includes explicit fields named `Failure:` and `Error details:` with the failing command, log, or concrete blocker. If a repository is not initialized after clone, use only gh CLI/git tools to create and push a main branch, then continue once git state is ready for work. Do not stop work just because you cannot create a pull request or watch remote CI/CD from inside this agent runtime. Finish repository changes and local validation you can perform; the harness will handle PR creation/update and remote check monitoring afterward.
readme-upkeep.commitMessage: docs: refresh README for clarity and accuracy
